title: Lithocarpus magnificus

intro: Lithocarpus magnificus, also known as the Himalayan Lithocarpus, is a species of evergreen tree in the family Fagaceae. The tree has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Indian Ayurvedic and Chinese medicine, where its extracts are reported to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial properties, which can aid in the treatment of various conditions such as fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Lithocarpus magnificus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Lithocarpus magnificus is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in the formulation of Ayurvedic and Chinese herbal remedies, such as churnas and granules.
